title: routing.rs: DtnMessage::size uses hardcoded overhead estimate
description:
File: rust/lichen-node/src/routing.rs:466-469

DtnMessage::size uses a hardcoded 100-byte overhead estimate for buffer accounting:

```rust
impl DtnMessage {
    pub fn size(&self) -> usize {
        self.packet.len() + 100 // header overhead estimate
    }
}
```

The actual overhead is much smaller - the struct has:
- packet: Vec<u8> (size already counted)
- destination_iid: [u8; 8] = 8 bytes
- expiry_unix: u32 = 4 bytes
- buffered_at_ms: u32 = 4 bytes
Total overhead: ~16 bytes + Vec heap overhead (~24 bytes) = ~40 bytes

Impact: Buffer accounting is ~2.5x too conservative. A 64KB DTN buffer configured to hold 1000 messages would only hold ~640 instead.

Fix: Use actual struct size: `self.packet.len() + core::mem::size_of::<DtnMessage>() - core::mem::size_of::<Vec<u8>>() + 24` or simplify to a more accurate constant like 50.
notes: Commit: bc765209 (size_of fix already applied, test updated to 350 for accuracy)
